name: Antigravity Health Watchdog
command: AG_CLI="${HOME}/.local/bin/antigravity-cli"; AG_STATUS=$($AG_CLI server status 2>&1); if [ $? -ne 0 ]; then echo "Antigravity DOWN: $AG_STATUS"; PGREP_COUNT=$(pgrep -f 'Antigravity' | wc -l | xargs); echo "Processes: $PGREP_COUNT"; if [ "$PGREP_COUNT" -lt 2 ]; then echo 'Attempting restart...'; open -a Antigravity; sleep 10; $AG_CLI server status 2>&1 && echo 'RECOVERED' || (echo 'STILL_DOWN'; curl -s -d '🚨 Antigravity failed to recover after restart on '$(scutil --get ComputerName)'' ntfy.sh/tonysM5; exit 1); else echo 'Antigravity process exists but CLI unresponsive — may need manual restart'; curl -s -d '⚠️ Antigravity unresponsive on '$(scutil --get ComputerName)' (process exists but CLI timeout)' ntfy.sh/tonysM5; exit 1; fi; else echo "Antigravity OK: $AG_STATUS"; fi
_comment: Checks Antigravity IDE health every 60s. Auto-restarts if down. Alerts via ntfy if restart fails.

name: System Health Check
command: echo '{"disk":"'$(df -h / | tail -1 | awk '{print $5}')'","load":"'$(uptime | awk -F'load averages:' '{print $2}' | xargs)'","memory_pressure":"'$(memory_pressure 2>/dev/null | tail -1 || echo 'n/a')'","bridges":{"telegram":"'$(curl -s -o /dev/null -w '%{http_code}' http://localhost:18791/status 2>/dev/null || echo 'down')'","teams":"'$(curl -s -o /dev/null -w '%{http_code}' --connect-timeout 3 http://localhost:3979/ 2>/dev/null || echo 'down')'"}}'
_comment: Basic system vitals — runs frequently, heals via JARVIS if something's wrong

name: Morning Brief
prompt:
Good morning. Please prepare Tony's morning brief:
1. Check today's calendar events
2. Review any overnight task results in hermitcrab/orchestrator/state.json
3. Surface any items from context/working-memory.md that need attention today
4. Send the brief to Tony via Telegram (/notify endpoint).
_comment: Daily morning brief — disabled until Tony wants to enable it

name: Bridge Watchdog
command: telegram=$(curl -s -o /dev/null -w '%{http_code}' --connect-timeout 3 http://localhost:18791/status 2>/dev/null); teams=$(curl -s -o /dev/null -w '%{http_code}' --connect-timeout 3 http://localhost:3979/ 2>/dev/null); if [ "$telegram" = "000" ] || [ "$teams" = "000" ]; then echo "ALERT: telegram=$telegram teams=$teams"; exit 1; else echo "OK: telegram=$telegram teams=$teams"; fi
_comment: Checks both bridges every 2 min. If either is down, L3 escalates to JARVIS to diagnose.

name: QMD Embedding
prompt: Run the QMD embedding workflow. Follow /qmd-embed workflow steps. Monitor progress and send ntfy notification when done.
_comment: QMD embedding — nocturnal task. Enable before bed, disable after. Has pre-flight checks.
